def solve(nums1: list[int]) -> bool:
    n = len(nums1)
    
    # Count of even (C0) and odd (C1) numbers in nums1
    c0 = 0
    c1 = 0
    for num in nums1:
        if num % 2 == 0:
            c0 += 1
        else:
            c1 += 1

    def check(P: int) -> bool:
        """
        Checks if it's possible to make all elements in nums2 have target parity P.
        P = 0 for even, P = 1 for odd.
        """
        for i in range(n):
            p_i = nums1[i] % 2

            # 1. Check direct choice: nums2[i] = nums1[i]
            if p_i == P:
                continue  # Success for this index i

            # 2. Direct choice failed. Check difference choice: nums2[i] = nums1[i] - nums1[j]
            
            # We need p(nums1[i]) XOR p(nums1[j]) = P
            # Required parity R for nums1[j] is R = P XOR p_i
            R = P ^ p_i

            can_find_j = False

            if p_i == R:
                # Case: p_i == R. We need another element j != i with parity p_i.
                # This requires C_p_i >= 2.
                C_R = c0 if R == 0 else c1
                if C_R >= 2:
                    can_find_j = True
            else:
                # Case: p_i != R. We need an element j != i with parity R.
                # Since R != p_i, any such element j must be different from i.
                # This requires C_R >= 1.
                C_R = c0 if R == 0 else c1
                if C_R >= 1:
                    can_find_j = True

            if not can_find_j:
                return False  # Cannot achieve parity P for index i
        
        return True  # Achievable for all i

    # Check if all can be made even (P=0) OR all can be made odd (P=1)
    return check(0) or check(1)
```
